title: --infra-name command line argument
body:
Adds the new --infra-name command-line argument allowing users to define the name of the infra container

Closes #10794

comments.body:
/approve
Couple nits, but looks solid overall
We have renamed the master branch to the main branch,   You need to rebase to the latest code base.

DCO validation is presently complaining that your commits do not have a valid signoff - you can add one with `git commit --amend -s` (easiest way is probably to squash the 4 commits into one, and then add a signoff)
Ah, manpage checker is noting that the new flag has not been added to docs/source/markdown/podman-pod-create.1.md
Test failures looked like infra, restarted

Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/OWNERS)~~ [edsantiago,mheon,rhatdan] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment
As far as I can see, the tests failed due time out error. Can we rerun them?
All system tests are failing, and those don't look like timeouts

> All system tests are failing, and those don't look like timeouts

Ops, my bad. There is typo in my last change without testing before push... =\
I just rebased the commit on top of the latest main branch. It seems Cirrus CI is having problems running the jobs.
